statement.p: The rectangular floor of your classroom is {9.25\ {\rm m}} longer than it is wide. The total floor area is {215\ {\rm m^{2}}}\text{.} How wide is the floor? (This is asking for the shorter dimension.)

solution.p: The area of a rectangle is given by multipying its two dimensions. If we let w stand for the width of the floor (the shorter dimension), then {w+9.25} is the length, and the total area is {w\mathopen{}\left(w+9.25\right)}\text{.} So we must solve the equation {w\mathopen{}\left(w+9.25\right)}=215\text{.}
After multiplying and moving to standard form, we have {w^{2}+9.25w-215}=0\text{.} Using the quadratic formula:
Only the solution h\approx{10.75} makes sense in this situation. The floor is {10.75\ {\rm m}} wide.
